NAMASTE Scheme
नमस्ते योजना
What does ‘NAMASTE’ stand for?
1) 'नमस्ते' क्या है?
NAMASTE is an abbreviation for “National Action for Mechanised Sanitation Ecosystem”.
नमस्ते "नेशनल एक्शन फॉर मेकेनाईज्ड सेनिटेशन इकोसिस्टम" का संक्षिप्त रूप है।
What is the objective of the NAMASTE scheme?
2) नमस्ते योजना का उद्देश्य क्या है?
Ensure safety and dignity of sanitation workers in urban India and enhancing their occupational safety through capacity building and improved access to PPT Kits, safety devices and machines.
In addition, NAMASTE would bring about a behavior change amongst citizens towards sanitation workers and enhance demand for safe sanitation services.
Now waste pickers have also been included in Namaste scheme from the current financial year and through the scheme, they will be included in the solid waste management process by ensuring their safety and dignity.
शहरी भारत में सीवर एवं सेप्टिक टैंक वर्कर (एसएसडब्लू) की सुरक्षा और गरिमा सुनिश्चित करना और क्षमता निर्माण और पीपीटी किट, सुरक्षा उपकरणों और मशीनों तक बेहतर पहुंच के माध्यम से उनकी व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा को बढ़ाना।
इसके अलावा, नमस्ते नागरिकों के बीच सीवर एवं सेप्टिक टैंक वर्कर (एसएसडब्लू) के प्रति व्यवहार में बदलाव लाएगा और सुरक्षित स्वच्छता सेवाओं की मांग को बढ़ाएगा।
अब नमस्ते योजना में कचरा बीनने वालों को भी जारी वित्त वर्ष से सम्मिलित कर लिया गया है तथा योजना के माध्यम से उनकी सुरक्षा एवं गरिमा सुनिश्चित करते हुए उन्हें सॉलिड वेस्ट मैनेजमेंट प्रक्रिया में सम्मिलित किया जाएगा
What are the intended outcomes of the NAMASTE scheme?
3) नमस्ते योजना के इच्छित परिणाम क्या हैं?
The intended outcomes of the NAMASTE scheme include zero fatalities in sanitation work, formalization and skilling of sanitation workers, elimination of direct contact with human faecal matter, establishment, strengthening and capacitating of Emergency Response Sanitation Units (ERSUs), and empowerment of sanitation workers through self-help groups and entrepreneurship, access to alternate livelihood options, and occupational safety training to all sewer and septic tank workers (SSWs).
नमस्ते योजना के इच्छित परिणामों में स्वच्छता कार्य में शून्य मृत्यु, सफाई कर्मचारियों का औपचारिकरण और कौशलप्रशिक्षण तथा, मानव-मलके साथ सीधे संपर्क को समाप्त करना, आपातकालीन प्रतिक्रिया स्वच्छता इकाइयों (ईआरएसयू) की स्थापना, सुदृढ़ीकरण और क्षमताकरण, और स्वयं सहायता समूहों और उद्यमिता के माध्यम से सफाई कर्मचारियों का सशक्तिकरण, वैकल्पिक आजीविका विकल्पों से जोडना और सभी सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंक कर्मचारियों (एसएसडब्ल्यू) के लिए व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा प्रशिक्षण शामिल हैं।
Who is a Sewer and Septic tank worker (SSW)?
4) सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंक कर्मचारी (एसएसडब्ल्यू) कौन है?
Sanitation workers who are engaged in cleaning and maintenance of sewers including those managing pumping stations, manholes/machine holes, and sewer lines; and those engaged in septic tank cleaning. Such workers may or may not be engaged by ULB/parastatal, empanelled/registered/licensed Private Sanitation Service Organisation (PSSO) and other private sanitation service providers.
ऐसे सफाई कर्मचारी जो सीवर, सेप्टिक टैंक मैनहोल/मशीन होल और सीवर लाइनों की सफाई तथा प्रबंधन सहित रखरखाव में लगे हुए हैं। ऐसे कर्मचारी यूएलबी/पैरास्टैटल, पैनलबद्ध/पंजीकृत/लाइसेंस प्राप्त निजी सफाई सेवा संगठन (पीएसएसओ) और अन्य निजी सफाई सेवा प्रदाताओं द्वारा नियोजित(और गैर नियोजित दोनो हो सकतें है।) हो भी सकते हैं और नहीं भी।
Who is a PSSO?
5) पीएसएसओ कौन है?
PSSO is Private Sanitation Service Organisation who are involved in cleaning and maintenance of sewer and septic tanks and may or may not be empanelled/registered/contracted/licensed with ULB.
पीएसएसओ एक निजी स्वच्छता सेवा संगठन है जो सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंकों की सफाई और रखरखाव में शामिल हैं पीएसएसओ यूएलबी के साथ सूचीबद्ध/ पंजीकृत / अनुबंधित / लाइसेंस प्राप्त हो सकता हैं और नहीं भी।
What is the role of State Government in implementation of NAMASTE scheme?
6) नमस्ते योजना के कार्यान्वयन में राज्य सरकार की क्या भूमिका है?
The role of State Government in implementation of NAMASTE is very important as State Government is to ensure the implementation of the Prohibition of Employment as Manual Scavangers and their Rehabilitation Act, 2013 which prohibits hazardous cleaning of sewer and septic tanks. The State should have to immediately facilitate and issue notification for setting up of Emergency Response Sanitation Unit (ERSU) and designate Responsible Sanitation Authority (RSA) in all Municipal Corporations and Districts with establishment of helpline number 14420. Further, necessary actions should be taken up by State Level Nodal Officer for NAMASTE to support convergence across the Swachh Bharat Mission (SBM), Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT), NAMASTE and Deendayal Antyoday Yojana – National Urban Livelihood Mission (DAY-NULM). The State has to nominate a State NAMASTE Nodal Officer, from the Urban Development Department, for implementation of NAMASTE.
नमस्ते योजना में राज्य सरकार की भूमिका बहुत महत्वपूर्ण है क्योंकि राज्य सरकार को हाथ से मैला उठाने वाले कर्मियों के नियोजन का प्रतिषेध और उनका पुनर्वास अधिनियम 2013,का कार्यान्वयन सुनिश्चित करना है जो सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंकों की जोखिमभरी सफाई कार्य मे लगे हुए है।राज्य के सभी जिलों के नगर निगमों मे आपातकालीन हेल्पलाइन नंबर 14420 की स्थापना और प्रतिक्रिया स्वच्छता इकाई (ईआरएसयू) और जिम्मेदार स्वच्छता प्राधिकरण (आरएसए) को नामित करने के लिए तत्काल सुविधा और अधिसूचना जारी करनी होगी। इसके अलावाराज्य स्तरीय नोडल अधिकारी द्वारा स्वच्छ भारत मिशन (एसबीएम), और शहरी परिवर्तन के लिए अटल मिशन (एएमआरयूटी), नमस्ते और दीनदयाल अंत्योदय योजना - राष्ट्रीय शहरी आजीविका मिशन (डीएवाई-एनयूएलएम) में अभिसरण का समर्थन करने के लिए आवश्यक कार्रवाई की जानी चाहिए।नमस्ते के कार्यान्वयन के लिए राज्य को शहरी विकास विभाग से एक राज्य नमस्ते नोडल अधिकारी नामित करना होगा।
7) What is the role of Urban Local Body (ULB) in implementation of NAMASTE scheme?
7) नमस्ते योजना के कार्यान्वयन में शहरी स्थानीय निकाय (यूएलबी) की क्या भूमिका है?
The role of ULB is nodal authority for implementation of NAMASTE scheme. ULBs will have to do enumeration of SSWs engaged by ULB/parastatal agencies and all PSSOs through profiling camps. Further, ULB shall facilitate occupational safety training to SSWs for the use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and safety gear along with handling of mechanized cleaning operations provided through NSKFDC.
यूएलबी योजना को लागू करने के लिएमुख्य प्राधिकृति है शहरी स्थानीय निकायों को प्रोफाइलिंग शिविरों के माध्यम से यूएलबी/पैरास्टेटल संस्थाओं और सभी पीएसएसओ द्वारा नियुक्त एसएसडब्ल्यू की गणना करनी होगी। इसके अलावा, यूएलबी एनएसकेएफडीसी के माध्यम से प्रदान किए गए मशीनीकृत सफाई कार्यों को संभालने के साथ-साथ व्यक्तिगत सुरक्षा उपकरण (पीपीई) और सुरक्षा गियर के उपयोग के लिए एसएसडब्ल्यू को व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा प्रशिक्षण की सुविधा प्रदान करेगा।
Additionally, ULB will have to support access to alternate livelihoods for SSWs through nominating SSW/dependents as beneficiary of SwachhtaUdyamiYojana (SUY) for linkage with capital subsidy to procure sanitation-related machines, provide long-term and assured mechanized cleaning work contract and mobilize SSW into SHG through linkage with NULM for sanitation related enterprise.
इसके अतिरिक्त, शहरी स्थानीय निकायों को स्वच्छता संबंधी मशीनों की खरीद के लिए पूंजीगत सब्सिडी के साथ संबधित मशीनों की खरीद, सफाई कार्य अनुबंध प्रदान करने और स्वच्छता संबंधी उद्यम के लिए एनयूएलएम के साथ संबंध के माध्यम से एसएसडब्ल्यू को एसएचजी में संगठित करने के लिए स्वच्छता उद्यमी योजना (एसयूवाई) के लाभार्थी के रूप में एसएसडब्ल्यू/आश्रितों को नामित करके एसएसडब्ल्यू के लिए वैकल्पिक आजीविकातक पहुंच का समर्थन करना होगा।
8) What kind of support will be provided to sanitation workers under the NAMASTE scheme?
8) नमस्ते योजना के तहत सफाई कर्मचारियों को किस तरह का समर्थन प्रदान किया जाएगा?
The scheme provides support in various forms, such as occupational safety training, provision of personal protective equipment (PPE), health insurance coverage, livelihood counselling and support, and access to subsidized loans for the purchase of sanitation-related equipment and linkage with other government schemes for them and their families.
यह योजना विभिन्न रूपों में सहायता प्रदान करती है, जैसे व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा प्रशिक्षण, व्यक्तिगत सुरक्षा उपकरण (पीपीई) का प्रावधान, स्वास्थ्य बीमा कवरेज, आजीविका परामर्श और समर्थन, और स्वच्छता से संबंधित उपकरणों की खरीद के लिए रियायती ऋण तक पहुंच और उनके परिवारों के लिए अन्य सरकारी योजनाओं के साथ जुड़ाव।
What if ULB is not the nodal authority for cleaning and maintenance of sewer lines and septic tanks?
9) क्या होगा यदि सीवर लाइनों और सेप्टिक टैंकों की सफाई और रखरखाव के लिए यूएलबी नोडल प्राधिकरण नहीं है, तो उस परिस्थिति में  क्या किया जाना चाहिए?
In this case, the ULB should consult the concerned parastatal agency/authority and collect the information of PSSOs, and SSWs working with them and collate the information. These workers must be informed for profiling camp.
ऐसी परिस्थिति में, यूएलबी को संबंधित पैरास्टेटल संस्थान / प्राधिकरण से परामर्श करना चाहिए और पीएसएसओ और उनके साथ काम करने वाले एसएसडब्ल्यू की जानकारी एकत्रित करके मिलान करना चाहिए। इन कर्मचारियों को प्रोफाइलिंग शिविर के लिए सूचित किया जाना चाहिए।
Under which condition manual entry is permitted for sewer or septic tank cleaning?
10) सीवर या सेप्टिक टैंक की सफाई के लिए किस परिस्थित में मानव प्रवेश की अनुमति है?
When the cleaning of sewer or septic tank is beyond capability of machines, only trained sanitation worker, properly equipped and authorised by RSA should be allowed to enter sewer or septic tank for cleaning.
जब सीवर या सेप्टिक टैंक की सफाई मशीनों से असंभव है, तब केवल सफाई कर्मचारी, जो आरएसए द्वारा सुसज्जित और अधिकृत है, उनको ही सीवर या सेप्टिक टैंक में प्रवेश करने की अनुमति दी जानी चाहिए।
As per MS Rules 2013, Chapter II, 3 (1)(e) “Any circumstance, when it is absolutely necessary to have manual sewage cleaning, after the CEO of the local authority has permitted to do so after recording in writing the specific valid reasons for allowing such cleaning”
एमएस नियम 2013, अध्याय II, 3 (1) (ई) के अनुसार, "कोई भी परिस्थिति, जब मैनुअल सीवेज सफाई करना अत्यंत आवश्यक है, स्थानीय प्राधिकरण के सीईओ द्वारा इस तरह की सफाई की अनुमति देने के लिए विशिष्ट वैध कारणों को लिखित रूप में दर्ज करने के बाद ऐसा करने की अनुमति दी गई है"।
3(2) “Any person engaged to clean a sewer or septic tank shall be provided by his employer, protective gear and safety devices”.
3(2) "सीवर या सेप्टिक टैंक की सफाई में लगे किसी भी व्यक्ति को उसके नियोक्ता, सुरक्षात्मक गियर और सुरक्षा उपकरणों द्वारा प्रदान किया जाएगा"।
Why is it important to focus on mechanization of sanitation work?
11) स्वच्छता कार्य के मशीनीकरण पर ध्यान केंद्रित करना क्यों महत्वपूर्ण है?
Sewer and septic tank Sanitation Workers (SSW) are directly exposed to hazardous gases like methane, hydrogen sulfide,carbon monoxide, faecal sludge and various occupational safety challenges while cleaning of sewers and septic tanks. Apart from increased risk of morbidity that these workers face due to their occupations, they face severe risks of losing their lives due to unsafe/un-regulated entry into sewer lines or septic tanks. This necessitates mechanization of sanitation work as mechanization eliminates sanitation worker’s direct contact with faecal matter. Use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and Safety Devices help in minimizing occupational hazards due to manual entry into sewers and septic tanks. ‘NAMASTE’ scheme aims to provide PPE kits (Personal Protective Equipment), safety devices and equipment for cleaning of sewer and septic tanks through ERSU.
सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंक सफाई कर्मचारी (एसएसडब्ल्यू)सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंकों की सफाई के दौरान मीथेन, हाइड्रोजन सल्फाइड, कार्बन मोनोऑक्साइड,जैसी जहरीली गैसों के संपर्क में आते है वमल कीचड़ और विभिन्न व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा चुनौतियों का सामना करतें है।वह अपने व्यवसायों के कारण इन कर्मचारियों को रुग्णता के बढ़ते जोखिम के अलावा, सीवर लाइनों या सेप्टिक टैंकों में असुरक्षित/ अनियमित प्रवेश के कारण जान का खतरा रहता है। उन्हें अपनी जान गंवाने के गंभीर जोखिम का सामना करना पड़ता है। इसके लिए स्वच्छता कार्य के मशीनीकरण की आवश्यकता होती है क्योंकि मशीनीकरण से सफाई कर्मचारी का मल के साथ सीधा संपर्क समाप्त हो जाता है। व्यक्तिगत सुरक्षा उपकरण (पीपीई) और सुरक्षा उपकरणों का उपयोग सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंकों में मैन्युअल प्रवेश के कारण व्यावसायिक खतरों को कम करने में मदद करता है। 'नमस्ते' योजना का उद्देश्य ईआरएसयू के माध्यम से सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंकों की सफाई के लिए पीपीई किट (व्यक्तिगत सुरक्षा उपकरण), सुरक्षा प्रदान करना है।
Emergency Response Sanitation Unit (ERSU)
आपातकालीन प्रतिक्रिया स्वच्छता इकाई (ईआरएसयू)
What is the objective of the ERSU?
12) ईआरएसयू का उद्देश्य क्या है?
The primary objective of the Emergency Response Sanitation Unit (ERUU) is to offer a professional, well-trained, motivated, and adequately equipped workforce for the maintenance and management of sewers and septic tanks. The aim is to eliminate fatalities resulting from workers entering sewers and septic tanks by providing them with proper personal protective equipment (PPE) and training. It is the responsibility of ERSU to supply safety devices and equipment for confined space entry to Sewer Entry Professionals (SEPs). ERSU is to be headed by the Responsible Sanitation Authority (RSA).
आपातकालीन प्रतिक्रिया स्वच्छता इकाई (ईआरयूयू) का प्राथमिक उद्देश्य सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंकों के रखरखाव और प्रबंधन के लिए एक पेशेवर, प्रशिक्षित,  और पर्याप्त रूप से सुसज्जित करना है। इस का उद्देश्य कर्मचारियों को उचित व्यक्तिगत सुरक्षा उपकरण पीपीई) और प्रशिक्षण प्रदान करके सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंकों में प्रवेश करने से होने वाली मौतों को खत्म करना है। सीवर एंट्री प्रोफेशनल्स (एसईपी) को सीमाबंध स्थान में प्रवेश के लिए सुरक्षा उपकरणों और उपकरणों की आपूर्ति करना ईआरएसयू की जिम्मेदारी है। ईआरएसयू का नेतृत्व जिम्मेदार स्वच्छता प्राधिकरण (आरएसए) द्वारा किया जाना है।
Who is an RSA?
13) आरएसए कौन है?
The Responsible Sanitation Authority (RSA) is designated as the District Magistrate or Municipal Commissioner of a major town or the regional head of a parastatal agency for their respective jurisdiction, including smaller clustered towns (ERSU Advisory, MoHUA, 2013). Their roles encompass ensuring timely service delivery, protecting sanitation workers, providing training, upgrades, equipment, and overseeing ERSU operations. In cases where human entry becomes necessary, the RSA assumes full authority and responsibility at the entry site until the task is deemed complete.
जिम्मेदार स्वच्छता प्राधिकरण (आरएसए) को एक प्रमुख शहर के जिला अधिकारी या नगर आयुक्त या एक पैरास्टेटल संस्था के क्षेत्रीय प्रमुख के रूप में नामित किया गया है, जिसमें छोटे क्लस्टर शहर भी शामिल हैं।(ईआरएसयू एडवाइजरी, एमओएचयूए, 2013)  उनकी भूमिकाओं में समय पर सेवा वितरण सुनिश्चित करना, स्वच्छता कर्मचारियों की रक्षा करना, प्रशिक्षण प्रदान करना, उन्नयन, उपकरण और ईआरएसयू संचालन की देखरेख करना शामिल है। ऐसे परिस्थिति में जहां मानव प्रवेश आवश्यक हो जाता है, आरएसए प्रवेश स्थल पर पूर्ण अधिकार और जिम्मेदारी लेता है जब तक कि कार्य पूरा नहीं माना जाता।
Where will ERSU be set up?
14) ईआरएसयू कहां स्थापित किया जाएगा?
Emergency Response Sanitation Unit (ERSU) will be set up in all Municipal Corporations and/or Water & Sewer Board and in capital cities of each State/UT, and at district headquarters. The ERSU shall be responsible to meet sanitation emergency requests from all smaller towns within a cluster say 75 km radius (ERSU Advisory, MoHUA,2019). 1100 ERSUs are envisaged to be set up across the country in Urban areas
आपातकालीन प्रतिक्रिया स्वच्छता इकाई (ईआरएसयू) सभी नगर निगमों और/या जल और सीवर बोर्ड और प्रत्येक राज्य/केंद्र शासित प्रदेश के राजधानी शहरों और जिला मुख्यालयों में स्थापित की जाएगी। ईआरएसयू 75 किलोमीटर के दायरे में सभी छोटे शहरों से स्वच्छता आपातकालीन अनुरोधों को पूरा करने के लिए जिम्मेदार होगा (ईआरएसयू एडवाइजरी, एमओएचयूए, 2019)। शहरी क्षेत्रों में देश भर में 1100 ईआरएसयू स्थापित करने की परिकल्पना की गई है।
Profiling Camp
प्रोफाइलिंग शिविर
What is a profiling camp? Why is a profiling camp conducted?
15) प्रोफाइलिंग शिविर क्या है? प्रोफाइलिंग शिविर क्यों आयोजित किया जाता है?
The  profiling camp is conducted to create a comprehensive digital database of Sewer and septic tank workers (SSWs) employed by ULBs, parastatal agencies, private sanitation service organizations (PSSOs), and other private operators.
प्रोफाइलिंग शिविर का आयोजन यूएलबी, पैरास्टेटल संस्थानों, निजी स्वच्छता सेवा संगठनों (पीएसएसओ) और अन्य निजी ऑपरेटरों द्वारा नियोजित सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंक कर्मचारियों(एसएसडब्ल्यू) का एक व्यापक डिजिटल डेटाबेस बनाने के लिए आयोजित किया जाता है।
It is evident that identifying the sanitation workers and connecting them with entitlements has been a challenge due to lack of survey database. Hence, it is important to create a national evidence base of sanitation workers to link workers and their families with NAMASTE scheme benefits. To create this database through enumeration on a digital platform, profiling camps are being conducted by ULBs.
यह स्पष्ट है कि सर्वेक्षण डेटाबेस की कमी के कारण सफाई कर्मचारियों की पहचान करना और उन्हें हकदारी (पात्रता) से जोड़ना एक चुनौती रही है। इसलिए, नमस्ते योजना के लाभों के साथ कर्मचारियोंऔर उनके परिवारों को जोड़ने के लिए स्वच्छता कर्मचारियोंका एक राष्ट्रीय साक्ष्य आधार बनाना महत्वपूर्ण है। डिजिटल प्लेटफॉर्म पर गणना के माध्यम से इस डेटाबेस को बनाने के लिए, यूएलबी द्वारा प्रोफाइलिंग शिविर आयोजित किए जा रहे हैं।
Who will conduct the profiling camp?
16) प्रोफाइलिंग शिविर का संचालन कौन करेगा?
Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) will organize city-level NAMASTE Camps. In the cities where the responsibility of providing sewer cleaning and septic tank is divided among ULB and the Parastatal agency, the ULB should coordinate with other departments such as PHED for conducting camps and if required seek support from the State Nodal NAMASTE Office
शहरी स्थानीय निकाय (यूएलबी) शहर स्तरीय नमस्ते शिविरों का आयोजन करेंगे। जिन शहरों में सीवर सफाई और सेप्टिक टैंक प्रदान करने की जिम्मेदारी यूएलबी और पैरास्टेटल संस्था के बीच विभाजित है, यूएलबी को शिविर आयोजित करने के लिए पीएचईडी जैसे अन्य विभागों के साथ समन्वय करना चाहिए। यदि आवश्यक हो तो राज्य नोडल नमस्ते कार्यालय से भी समर्थन ले सकतें है।
What is the role of ULB in organizing a profiling camp?
17) प्रोफाइलिंग शिविर के आयोजन में यूएलबी की क्या भूमिका है?
The ULBs will assume responsibility for gathering data for the profiling of SSWs and their families, utilizing a mobile app or web portal.
यूएलबी मोबाइल ऐप या वेब पोर्टल का उपयोग करके एसएसडब्ल्यू और उनके परिवारों की प्रोफाइलिंग के लिए डेटा एकत्र करने की जिम्मेदारी लेंगे।
The role of the ULB in profiling of SSW is as follows:
एसएसडब्ल्यू की प्रोफाइलिंग में यूएलबी की भूमिका इस प्रकार है:
Nominate a ULB Nodal Officer.
● एक यूएलबी नोडल अधिकारी नामित करना।
Collect basic details of SSWs employed by the ULB, parastatal agencies and PSSOs through NAMASTE portal.
Nominate enumerators and validators to carry out digital enumeration process and create their user id on NAMASTE App for enumeration.
● डिजिटल गणना प्रक्रिया को पूरा करने के लिएप्रगणकों और सत्यापनकर्ताओं को नामित करें और गणना के लिए नमस्ते ऐप पर अपनी उपयोगकर्ता आईडी बनाएं ।
Conduct IEC through posters, hoardings in local languages for workers to know of the camp date and locations.
● शिविर की तारीख और स्थानों के बारे में जागरुकता बढाने के लिए कर्मचारियों के लिए स्थानीय भाषाओं में पोस्टर, होर्डिंग के माध्यम से आईईसी का संचालन करें।
What should be the time of a profiling camp?
18) प्रोफाइलिंग शिविर का समय क्या होना चाहिए?
The camps can be scheduled at such times that it is convenient for the SSWs and PSSOs to attend and does not impact their wages for the day. For example, camps for ULB staff may be conducted during the day and for SSWs working for PSSO or other private contractors may be scheduled in the evenings.
शिविर ऐसे समय पर अनुसूचित करेंजिससे एसएसडब्ल्यू और पीएसएसओ भाग लेना सुविधाजनकरहें और एसएसडब्ल्यू केदिन भर के वेतन पर कोई प्रभाव न पड़े। उदाहरण के लिए, यूएलबी कर्मचारियों के लिए शिविर दिन के दौरान आयोजित किए जा सकते हैं और पीएसएसओ या अन्य निजी ठेकेदारों के लिए काम कर रहे एसएसडब्ल्यू के लिए शाम को अनुसूचित किए जा सकते हैं।
Who is this survey for? Who are the beneficiaries?
19) यह सर्वेक्षण किसके लिए है? लाभार्थी कौन हैं?
The scheme focuses on Sewer and Septic Tank Workers (SSWs) who are engaged in either (a) cleaning of sewer network/ machine holes and/ or (b) emptying of septic tanks/ pits. SSWs may be employed by ULBs, parastatal agencies, PSSOs, state-level departments and other private operators. SSWs are the direct beneficiaries of the scheme.
यह योजना सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंक कर्मचारियों(एसएसडब्ल्यू) पर केंद्रित है जो सीवर नेटवर्क / मशीन घेल की सफाई और / या  सेप्टिक टैंक / गड्ढों की सफाई में लगे हुए हैं। एसएसडब्ल्यू को यूएलबी, पैरास्टेटल संस्थाओं, पीएसएसओ, राज्य-स्तरीय विभागों और अन्य निजी ऑपरेटरों द्वारा नियोजित किया जा सकता है। एसएसडब्ल्यू इस योजना के प्रत्यक्ष लाभार्थी हैं।
How will the NAMASTE scheme address the occupational health risks faced by sanitation workers?
20) नमस्ते योजना स्वच्छता कर्मचारियों द्वारा सामना किए जाने वाले व्यावसायिक स्वास्थ्य जोखिमों को कैसे संबोधित करेगी?
The scheme aims to enhance the occupational safety of sanitation workers by providing personal protective equipment (PPE) kits, such as gloves, body suits, masks, and safety goggles etc. These measures help protect sanitation workers from any diseases/hazards associated with their work.
इस योजना का उद्देश्य व्यक्तिगत सुरक्षा उपकरण (पीपीई) किट, जैसे दस्ताने, बॉडी सूट, मास्क और सुरक्षा चश्मे आदि प्रदान करके स्वच्छता कर्मचारियों की व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा को बढ़ाना है। ये उपाय सफाई कर्मचारियों को उनके काम से जुड़ी किसी भी बीमारी/खतरे से बचाने में मदद करते हैं।
Will the NAMASTE scheme provide training for sanitation workers?
21) क्या नमस्ते योजना सफाई कर्मचारियों के लिए प्रशिक्षण प्रदान करेगी?
Yes, the NAMASTE scheme includes provisions for training sanitation workers. It focuses on skill-building, capacity development, and occupational safety training. This helps enhance the professionalism and expertise of sanitation workers.
हां, नमस्ते योजना में सफाई कर्मचारियों को प्रशिक्षित करने के प्रावधान शामिल हैं। यह कौशल-निर्माण, क्षमता विकास और व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा प्रशिक्षण पर केंद्रित है। यह सफाई कर्मचारियों की व्यावसायिकता और विशेषज्ञता को बढ़ाने में मदद करता है।
What kind of assistance is provided to SSWs for sanitation related projects under ‘NAMASTE’ scheme?
22) 'नमस्ते' योजना के तहत स्वच्छता से संबंधित परियोजनाओं के लिए सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंक कर्मचारियों को किस प्रकार की सहायता प्रदान की जाती है?
The capital subsidy is provided by way of upfront capital subsidy to SSWs.
पूंजीगत सब्सिडी सीवर और सेप्टिक टैंक कर्मचारियोंको अग्रिम पूंजीगत सब्सिडी के माध्यम से प्रदान की जाती है।

Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      1 
1. Who is this manual for?  
This user manual is primarily designed for District & Block Nodal Officers, surveyors, resource 
organizations, etc., engaged in profiling of waste pickers. 
 Surveyors are responsible for conducting surveys of waste pickers.  
 Block Nodal Officers are responsible for error flagging, validation, and cr eating user IDs for 
surveyors/validators. 
 Block Nodal Officers may nominate validators to validate the profiled waste pickers if the 
number of identified waste pickers is high. 
 District Nodal Officer are responsible for creating user IDs for Block Nodal Officer and the final 
validation of the profiled waste pickers at District Level. 
2.  Objective of this manual  
 To provide clear guidance on using mobile application and portal for survey, data entry and 
submission.  
 To explain the process for identifying, flagging, and resolving data errors.  
 To describe data validation procedures and role-based access responsibilities. 
3. Whom to survey?  
Explaining the Target Group for Waste Picker Survey

(plastic, paper, e-waste, glass, metal, hair or any

4. Roles & Responsibilities of Nodal Officers & Master Trainers 
Role of the District Nodal Officer 
 The District Nodal Officer will be responsible for nomination of Master Trainer at District 
level and the nomination of Block Nodal Officer. 
 Creation of user IDs for Block Nodal Officer 
Role of the Master Trainer 
 Preparation of training delivery plan for training of Block Nodal Officers & the surveyors 
 Master Trainer will be responsible to conduct the training of Block & Gram Panchayat level 
functionaries on profiling through NAMASTE Waste Picker application and portal. 
Role of the Block Nodal Officer 
 The Block Nodal Officer will be responsible for nomination of surveyors at the Panchayat. 
 Creation of user IDs for Surveyor/V alidators. 
5. Conducting surveys in rural areas  
The surveyors can be from panchayat functionaries, resource organizations empaneled by NSKFDC, 
and if required panchayats may seek support from SHGs (self -help groups) and civil society 
organizations to meet the adequacy of survey requirements. 
Functional mobile/computer literacy is a must for the selected surveyors to use the Waste Pickers 
mobile application or the Waste Pickers web portal.  The Master Trainer, trained by NSKFDC on the use 
of the survey application and portal, will be responsible for conducting training sessions for Block and 
Panchayat Level Nodal Officers and Surveyors. 
5.1 Role of the Surveyor 
 The surveyor will be responsible for profiling Waste Pickers on the mobile application/ portal. 
 The surveyor must facilitate the waste picker or waste worker to go to the nearest Aadhaar 
center to get an Aadhaar card or update an existing one. 
 The surveyor is expected to survey at least 10 to 15 waste pickers every day and report the 
progress of the survey to the ULB officials and NSKFDC regularly.  
 After error flagging (Quality Checks), the surveyor  will be responsible for rectifying all errors 
within a week.  
 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      3 
5.2 What does a surveyor need for the survey? 
The survey can be done either through the Waste Pickers mobile application or portal using 
laptops/desktops.  
For ease of taking photographs, it is recommended to use the mobile application. 
Checklist for Waste Picker Survey  
 Checklist for Survey on Mobile Application  Checklist for Survey on Desktop/Laptop 
 Use an Android smartphone and keep 
your charger handy. 
 Download the latest version of the 
survey app from the Google Play Store. 
 If the app prompts an update, please 
update it to the latest version. 
 Make sure your phone is fully charged 
to avoid interruptions. 
 Ensure you have an internet 
connection while entering geo-division 
details and syncing the survey. 
 Use the login ID and password 
provided by ULB. 
 Use a desktop or laptop with Windows 10 or a 
newer operating system, ensuring it is 
connected to a charger and a stable power 
source. 
 Ensure a stable internet connection and use an 
updated web browser (Chrome 115, Firefox 
72, or Edge 115). 
 Ensure to have a webcam ready for capturing 
respondent images. 
 Ensure you have a document scanner available 
for scanning any necessary documents. 
 Use the login ID and password provided by 
ULB. 
Do’s for Surveyors while Using the App/Portal 
 Always use the latest version of the app to avoid any technical issues.  
 Keep your username and password confidential to protect survey data. 
 Make sure all required fields are filled out before submitting the survey. 
 Always provide accurate information and double-check all the details before submitting the 
survey.  
 Read and follow the app’s on-screen instructions to ensure accurate data collection. 
 Once you have completed the survey, make sure to sync it properly to save and submit the 
survey for the next steps. 
 The app requires GPS to record accurate location details. Ensure your device’s location 
services are turned on. 
 If your location services are off, follow these simple steps to turn them on:  
 Swipe down from the top of your screen to open the notification panel. 
 Search for the “location” option in the notification panel by scrolling down/ sideways, 
whichever may be applicable.  
 Click on the “location” option; this will activate the GPS/ location service on the device.  
 Ensure your work is saved before exiting the app to prevent data loss. 
 Make sure to give all required permissions if the portal or application asks for them. 
 
Tips for the smooth functioning of the Application 
Tips to improve the performance of the mobile device while conducting the survey: 
 Turn off Bluetooth and Wi-Fi when not in use. 
 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      4 
 Ensure that unnecessary apps are closed in the background to help conserve your phone's 
battery. 
 Keep the phone in silent mode to minimize distractions during the survey. 
 Clear cache memory regularly to free up space and enhance performance. 
 
Tips for the smooth functioning of the Portal 
Tips to improve the performance of desktop/laptops while conducting the survey: 
 Avoid keeping multiple tabs open while using the survey portal to prevent slowdowns. 
 Ensure that the laptop/ desktop operating systems are updated. 
 
4.1 Guiding principles for surveyors  
Do not make false promises and raise expectations of waste pickers 
Do not make unrealistic promises to the waste pickers. Explain to them about the scheme and the 
benefits associated with the scheme and inform them that while this survey will help in the enrolment 
of entitlements under the scheme, the appropriate department from the ULB will act for other needs.  
Empathy  
The survey includes capturing personal and sensitive information from the beneficiaries. The 
Surveyors should be mindful of this fact and respect their choices and decisions. Maintaining a polite 
and positive attitude for this survey is essential. Ensure that waste pickers are comfortably seated 
during the survey. 
Privacy  
The Surveyors must respect the privacy of the data they collect, and it must be used only for profiling 
purposes. No leakage of photos or information will be tolerated. 
5 Survey Process Flow  
The survey process for rural areas follows a structured flow, involving key steps such as survey, error 
flagging, error resolution, and validation.  Rural areas require two level of validation (At Block & 
District Level). This process ensures an accurate survey of waste pickers, with the involvement of 
District & Block nodal officers and validators.  
The survey process is as follows: 
1. User Creation and Engagement 
NSKFDC will provide unique IDs to District Nodal Officers, who will generate login credentials  
for Block Nodal Officers. Block Nodal Officers will then create credentials for surveyors and 
validators. 
 
2. Conducting the Survey including eKYC of Aadhar IDs of waste pickers 
 Survey questionnaire includes questions related to eKYC of Aadhar IDs for valida ting the 
waste pickers. (refer section 8.2) 
 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      5 
 eKYC process in the application, is to ensure digital verification process to authenticate 
user identity using Aadhaar and Aadhaar-linked mobile number. The process involves OTP-
based authentication, providing a reliable and efficient way to verify personal information. 
3. Error Flagging 
 All submitted surveys will be reviewed by the Block Nodal Officer/ Validators to check for 
inconsistencies, missing information, or errors. If issues are found, they will flag the related 
questions which the surveyor will need to rectify within time span of 7 days. 
 It is recommended that the Block nodal officer review the completed surveys within 7 days 
of survey completion. 
 Additionally, NSKFDC and NAMASTE PMU w ill conduct a  quality check of 10% of all 
completed surveys to ensure quality rigor.  
4. Error Resolution 
 Once errors are flagged, the surveyor will revisit the survey data to resolve the flagged 
errors. This may involve revisiting the waste picker and updati ng their information and 
seeking additional information as required to ensure accuracy. Surveyors may call the 
waste picker to the Block/Panchayat office or conduct field visits.  
 The errors flagged must be resolved by the surveyors within 7 days from the day on which 
they were flagged.  
 It is recommended that the surveyor resolve the flagged errors within 7 days of receiving 
the errors. 
 
5. Validation 
 It is recommended that the Block Nodal Officer will validate the completed surveys 
within 7 days of the error resolution. 
 Please note that for rural areas there will be two levels of validation at the Block & District 
level.  
6. Waste pickers' ID generation and ID card distribution 
 After validation, a unique NAMASTE ID will be generated for each validated waste picker, 
recognizing them under the NAMASTE scheme. 
 The Block Nodal Officer will distribute Waste Picker ID cards to all validated waste pickers, 
completing the formal recognition process.

8.2 Filling the survey forms and understanding the survey questionnaire 
The survey form is broadly divided into 4 sections: 
1. General Information  
This section gathers survey location and personal details of the worker, such as name, gender, date 
of birth and Aadhar card. 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      16 
2. Job Role  
This section focuses on the waste picker job role information, type of employment,  employer 
details, and other information. 
3. Socio-economic Details  
This section collects contact information, family details, residential address, educational 
background, and social profile of the worker. 
4. Entitlement Linkage  
This section inquires whether the worker is linked to any government entitlements or benefits.

 Always capture a photo in good light. Make sure 
that the light is facing the participant. Switch on 
the lights in the room if it is evening time.  
 It is preferable if the background is not dark or 
messy. Look for a light and clean background.  
 Ask the respondent to stand straight and look

picture. 
 The distance between the camera and the 
respondent should be at least 1 meter (3 feet).  
 The photograph frame should contain the top of 
the head, the ends of both shoulders and the 
person’s waist.  
 Keep the camera/phone at eye level.  
 Click on the shutter once without moving your 
hands.  Check if the photo is clear .

cropped.

eKYC Scenarios 1:  
If the respondent has their Aadhar card and has the OTP received on the mobile number linked 
with Aadhar, the eKYC process can be completed successfully. In this case, the surveyor should 
select the option a “Yes”, which means the Aadhar card is available in aadhar question (Q3). 
 
 
Figure 1 eKYC Scenario 1: Aadhar card is available, and eKYC is completed 
eKYC Scenarios 2:  
If the respondent has their Aadhar card with them, but details do not match (like photo, DOB), 
then the surveyor will reject the eKYC verification. 
After rejection, the surveyor must select either option b, “No,” or option c, “Yes, but currently not 
available,” if ID details do not match in Aadhar question (Q3). In such cases, it is mandatory to 
select another ID proof to successfully complete the survey.  
Please note that if neither Aadhaar nor another ID proof is available, the survey cannot be 
submitted. 
Completion of eKYC is a mandatory prerequisite for respondents to proceed with the next steps of 
the survey process and become eligible to avail the benefits of the scheme. So please note that in 
such cases, the ULB/RO surveyor should assist the workers in updating their Aadhar details or, if 
they do not have an Aadhar card, help them obtain one from the nearest Aadhar center.  

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      22 
 
Figure 2 eKYC Scenario 2: Aadhar card is available, but details do not match, so eKYC is not successful. 
eKYC Scenarios 3:  
If the respondent has their Aadhaar card but the eKYC process fails because the respondent 
didn’t receive the OTP , as the mobile number linked to the Aadhaar is not currently accessible to 
the respondent, Aadhaar-based verification cannot be completed. So, the surveyor must select 
option c, “Yes, but currently not available” in the Aadhar question (Q3).  
In such cases, it is mandatory to select another ID proof to complete the survey.  
Please note that if neither Aadhaar nor another ID proof is available, the survey cannot be 
submitted. 
Completion of eKYC is a mandatory prerequisite for respondents to proceed with the next steps of 
the survey process and become eligible to avail the benefits of the scheme. So please note that in 
such cases, the ULB/RO surveyor should assist the workers in updating their Aadhar details or, if 
they do not have an Aadhar card, help them obtain one from the nearest Aadhar center.  

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      23 
 
Figure 3 eKYC Scenario 3: Aadhar card is available, but eKYC is not successful as OTP was not received. 
eKYC Scenarios 4:  
If the respondent's Aadhar card is currently not available, but other government-issued ID proof 
is available.  
So, the surveyor must select option b, “No” which means Aadhar is not available in the Aadhar 
question (Q3). 
In such cases, it is mandatory to select another ID proof in Q4 to complete the survey.  
Please note that if neither Aadhaar nor another ID proof is available, the survey cannot be 
submitted. 
Completion of eKYC is a mandatory prerequisite for respondents to proceed with the next steps of 
the survey process and become eligible to avail the benefits of the scheme. So please note that in 
such cases, the ULB/RO surveyor should assist the workers in updating their Aadhar details or, if 
they do not have an Aadhar card, help them obtain one from the nearest Aadhar center.  

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      24 
 
Figure 4 eKYC Scenario 4: Aadhar card is currently not available, but other government-issued ID proof is available.  
eKYC Scenarios 5:  
If the respondent's Aadhar card and other government-issued ID proof are not available. 
So, the surveyor must select option b, “No” which means Aadhar is not available in the Aadhar 
question (Q3) and none in the other ID proof question (Q4). 
Please note that if neither Aadhaar nor another ID proof is available, the survey cannot be 
submitted. It is mandatory to have any ID proof to complete the filling of the survey. 
 
Figure 5 eKYC Scenario 5: Aadhar card and other government-issued ID proof is not available.

Self-Help Groups (SHGs) are groups of 10 to 20 
women or men who come together to improve 
their living conditions by group savings and loans. 
These groups conduct regular meetings where the 
savings of the group are collected into a corpus 
fund, which is used to provide short-term loans to 
the members. After some time when the credit 
requirements of the members increase, SHG may 
approach a bank for the loan.  
 Ask the respondent if they are a member of 
SHG, if they say “Yes”, then nudge whether they 
have received a revolving fund. ask for the 
name of their SHG, and then determine 
whether to select “yes” or not.

 Ask the workers what schemes they and/or 
their family members have availed and select 
the appropriate schemes.  
 Pradhan Mantri Awas Y ojana (PMAY): A 
housing scheme that provides affordable 
housing for all, targeting urban and rural 
populations with financial assistance for 
home construction or improvement. 
 Education Support: Financial assistance and 
resources to promote access to quality 
education. 
 Pre-Matric Scholarship (MoSJE): A 
scholarship scheme by the Ministry of 
Social Justice and Empowerment for 
students from economically weaker 
sections to support their education before 
matriculation. 
 Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan 
Arogya Y ojana (AB-PMJAY): A healthcare 
scheme providing health insurance 
coverage up to ₹5 lakh per family annually 
for secondary and tertiary hospitalization. 
 e-SHRAM registration: A national database 
for unorganized workers to ensure social 
security and welfare benefits for laborers 
and informal sector workers. 
 One Nation One Ration Card Scheme 
(ONORC): A scheme that allows 
beneficiaries to access subsidized food 
grains from any fair-price shop across India, 
regardless of their state of residence. 
 Sukanya Samriddhi Y ojana (SSY): A savings 
scheme aimed at securing the financial 
future of girl children by offering high 
interest rates on deposits. 
 Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Y ojana 
(PMSBY): An accident insurance scheme 
offering coverage for death or disability due 
to accidents at a low premium. 
 Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Y ojana 
(PMJJBY): A life insurance scheme providing 
coverage for death at a low premium, 
targeted at the economically weaker 
sections. 
 National Urban Livelihoods Mission 
(NULM): A mission that aims to reduce 
poverty and vulnerability among urban 
 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      39 
S. No. Question Explanation 
poor through employment generation and 
skill development. 
 Pradhan Mantri Kaushalya Vikas Y ojana 
(PMKVY): A skill development initiative 
providing short-term training and 
certification to improve the employability 
of youth across various sectors. 
35. Photo of waste picker. * (Image 
upload) 
 Click a clear, solo image of the respondent, with 
a clean background. 
 Things to remember while taking a photo of the 
respondent.  
 Always capture a photo in good light. Make 
sure that the light is facing the respondent. 
Switch on the lights in the room if it is 
evening time.  
 It is preferable if the background is not dark 
or messy. Look for a light and clean 
background.  
 Ask the respondent to stand straight and 
look into the camera lens.  
 The distance between the camera and the 
respondent should be at least 1 meter (3 
feet).  
 The photograph frame should contain the 
top of the head, the ends of both shoulders 
and the person’s waist.  
 Keep the camera/phone at eye level.  
 Click on the shutter once without moving 
your hands. o Check if the photo is clear .  
 Click a picture again if it is blurred, dark or 
cropped.

9 Error flagging  
Error Flagging is the process of checking the data collected during the  survey and identifying critical 
errors that may affect respondent validation or eligibility for various schemes . After a survey is 
submitted, it undergoes an initial quality check (QC ) to identify any errors, such as missing consent, 
incomplete fields, or incorrect document uploads. The nodal officer or validator at the ULB level will 
review the data collected by surveyors and flag any identified errors. It will be the responsibility of the 
surveyors to rectify the errors.   
Please ensure the following things while doing the error flagging- 
 Ensure audio consent is properly recorded. 
 Verify that all required sections are completed, especially conditional questions based on 
previous responses. 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      44 
 Cross-check key identifiers, such as Aadhaar card details and employment information, for 
accuracy. 
 Review the clarity and quality of the uploaded images for supporting documents. 
 Confirm that the appropriate supporting documents are uploaded based o n the options 
selected. 
 If the Aadhaar card is uploaded, cross-check that the name, date of birth, and address match 
the provided information. 
 Check the details of family members, including name, age, relation, and Aadhaar numbers. 
 Ensure the waste picker's photo is clear and accurately represents the individual.

authority and tap 
‘LOGIN’. 
 
 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      45 
S. No. Steps Image 
2.  After logging into the 
application, you will be 
directed to the main 
page. 
Here, you will find links 
to the FAQs, User 
Manual, and Tutorial 
Videos—feel free to 
refer to them if you 
have any questions. 
 
 
3.  By clicking on 'My 
Dashboard', you will be 
redirected to the 
homepage, where you 
can view the status of 
all the surveys 
completed by you. 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      46 
S. No. Steps Image 
4.  Click on "QC Raise" to 
view the QC page. 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      47 
S. No. Steps Image 
5.  The QC page displays all 
surveys that require a 
quality check. 
Each Survey has a 
download Icon. 
Click the download icon 
to enable the survey for 
error flagging. 
 
 
6.  After clicking the 
download icon, the 
editing icon will come, 
click on it and you will 
be able to flag the 
errors.  
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      48 
S. No. Steps Image 
7.  The filled survey form 
will open.  
Each question will have 
a red “i” icon. 
 
8.  In the job role section, 
the questions  
"Where do you collect 
waste?" and  
"Do you have access to 
employee benefits such 
as PF or ESI?"  
are mandatory and have 
two icons, a red “i” and 
a “green tick mark”.  
 
If there are any errors, 
click on the red “i” icon. 
If there are no errors, 
click on the “green tick 
mark”.  
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      49 
S. No. Steps Image 
9.  If there are any errors in 
the survey, then click on 
red “i” and this will 
open a pop-up box to 
type in remarks. 
 
After entering the 
remarks, click on the 
“Raise QC” and move to 
the next question to 
mark QC. 
 
 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      50 
S. No. Steps Image 
10.  After entering the 
remarks, click on the 
“Submit”. 
 
If there are no errors in 
the filled survey after 
thoroughly checking it, 
simply click on the 
submit button at the 
end. 
 
 
11.  After submitting, you 
will be directed to the 
QC Raise Page and, on 
each survey where 
errors are flagged, you 
will see two icons, “Sync 
and Edit”. 
 
Click the edit icon if 
some corrections are 
pending. 
 
Click sync to submit the 
form for the next step 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      51 
S. No. Steps Image 
12.  After getting synced the 
status of the survey will 
change to QC Raised on 
the survey page. 
 
 
This marks the completion of the error flagging. 
After the errors are flagged, the surveyor is responsible for correcting these errors. 
Please note that once the surveyor resolves the flagged issues, the survey is resubmitted for another 
QC round. If any errors remain, the survey is flagged again and returned for further corrections. This 
cycle continues until all errors are resolved. If no errors are found, the survey moves to the validation 
stage, where the data is further assessed for final confirmation before analysis. This process ensures 
that only high-quality and accurate data proceeds to the next stage. 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      52 
10 Error resolving  
The surveyor is responsible for correcting these errors, which may involve revisiting the respondent or 
re-uploading documents. This may involve contacting the waste pickers through field visits or inviting 
them to the ULB office to resolve the errors. 
S. No. Steps Image 
1.  Login using the Surveyor 
user ID and password 
Use the login ID and 
password as provided by 
the Local authority and 
tap ‘LOGIN’. 
 
 
 
2.  By clicking on 'My 
Dashboard', you will be 
redirected to the 
homepage, where you 
can view the status of all 
the surveys completed by 
you. 
 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      53 
S. No. Steps Image 
3.  After coming to the home 
page, click on the QC 
resolve. 
 
 
4.  Click on the QC resolve, 
and the QC page will 
display all the error-
flagged surveys. 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      54 
S. No. Steps Image 
5.  Click the download icon 
to enable the survey for 
editing. 
 
 
 
 
6.  When the editing sign 
comes, click on it, and 
you will be able to edit 
the form.  
 
All errors flagged in the 
survey need to be 
resolved. 
 
 
 
 
7.  Only error-flagged 
questions will be 
displayed.  
Each error flagged 
question will have two 
icons: a red flag and a 
green tick mark. 
 
 
 
8.  Click on the red flag to 
view the comment or 
reason for the error being 
flagged. 
After reading the 
comment, click anywhere 
on the screen and 
proceed to edit. 
 
Edit the answers as per 
the comments and click 
on the Green flag. 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      55 
S. No. Steps Image 
 
 
 
9.  Once all the errors are 
resolved, click on the 
“SUBMIT”. 
 
10.  When back on the list of 
QC Resolve page, click the 
“sync icon” to submit the 
survey for next step. 
 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      56 
S. No. Steps Image 
11.  After getting synced the 
status of the survey will 
change to QC Resolved 
on the survey page. 
 
 
This marks the completion of the Error Resolving Process 
All the errors resolved will be reviewed by the validator. If the validator confirms that all necessary 
changes have been made and approves the form without flagging in any error, the survey will proceed 
to the next stage—validation at the local authority level. 
11 First Level of Validation 
To ensure that all waste pickers receive the benefits of schemes, it is essential to survey and validate 
them through local authorities. The validation process, managed by the local authority, confirms 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      57 
whether the surveyed individuals are genuine waste pickers. In rural areas, two level of validation will 
be there to certify the waste pickers  
S. No. Steps Image 
1.  Login using the user ID and 
password 
Use the login ID and password 
as provided by the NSKFDC or 
Local authority and tap 
‘LOGIN’. 
 
 
 
2. After logging into the 
application, you will be 
directed to the main page. 
Click on 'My Dashboard', you 
will be redirected to the 
homepage, where you can 
view the status of all the 
surveys completed by you. 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      58 
S. No. Steps Image 
3. To validate the surveys click on 
"Validate"  
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      59 
S. No. Steps Image 
4. The Validate Page displays all 
surveys that are submitted for 
validation. 
Each survey has a download 
and view Icon. 
 
 
5. Click on the “eye icon’ to view 
the survey. 
 
Check the survey properly, and 
if there is any incorrect 
information, you can invalidate 
the worker. 
 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      60 
S. No. Steps Image 
 
6. Click the download icon to 
enable the survey for editing. 
 
 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      61 
S. No. Steps Image 
7. When the editing sign comes, 
you will be able to validate the 
survey. 
 
 
 
8. The worker will be validated 
based on two categories:  
Name and Job Role. 
 
Each question will have a valid 
and invalid option with a set of 
reasons to select from. 
 
 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      62 
S. No. Steps Image 
9. Validation 
Questions: Waste picker Name  
 
 To validate the workers, 
click on “Valid”  
 Select the appropriate 
reason from the drop-
down to validate  
 To invalidate the workers, 
click on “Invalid”  
 Select the appropriate 
reason from the drop-
down to invalidate. 
 
(The detailed validation criteria 
are provided in Annexure I).  
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      63 
S. No. Steps Image 
10. Validation 
Questions: Have you been 
engaged in waste picking? 
 
 To validate the workers, 
click on “Valid”  
 Select the appropriate 
reason from the drop-
down to validate  
 To invalidate the workers, 
click on “Invalid”  
 Select the appropriate 
reason from the drop-
down to invalidate. 
 
(The detailed validation criteria 
are provided in Annexure I).  
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      64 
S. No. Steps Image 
11. Once validated or invalidated, 
click on the submit button. 
 
Please note that If in any 
question the worker is marked 
invalid, then he will be 
considered invalidated and not 
be considered for further 
process.  
 
 
12. When back on validate workers 
page, click on the sync button. 
 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      65 
S. No. Steps Image 
13. After getting synced the status 
of the survey will change to 
validated on the survey page. 
 
 
 
 
 
This marks the completion of the first level of validation process for rural areas. 
 
After the first level of validation  at block level, the next step is to validate the survey at the district 
level. 
12 Second Level of Validation 
The final step of the survey process in rural areas is to validate the waste pickers or waste workers at 
the district level. This ensures the accuracy and reliability of the collected data. This will be done by 
the district-level validator . 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      66 
To validate, follow the following steps: 
S. No. Steps Image 
1.  Login using the user ID and 
password 
Use the login ID and password 
as provided by the NSKFDC or 
Local authority and tap 
‘LOGIN’. 
 
 
 
2. After logging into the 
application, you will be 
directed to the main page. 
Click on 'My Dashboard', you 
will be redirected to the 
homepage, where you can 
view the status of all the 
surveys completed by you. 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      67 
S. No. Steps Image 
3. To validate the surveys, click 
on "Validate"  
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      68 
S. No. Steps Image 
4. The Validate Page displays all 
surveys that are submitted for 
validation. 
Each survey has a download 
and view Icon. 
 
 
5. Click on the “eye icon’ to view 
the survey. 
 
Check the survey properly, and 
if there is any incorrect 
information, you can invalidate 
the worker. 
 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      69 
S. No. Steps Image 
 
6. Click the download icon to 
enable the survey for editing. 
 
 
 
7. When the editing sign comes, 
you will be able to validate the 
survey. 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      70 
S. No. Steps Image 
 
 
8. When you click on edit, a pop-
up titled "District Validation" 
will appear. 
The message will ask: "Do you 
want to validate this survey at 
the district level?" 
Choose one of the following 
options: 
 Y es (green button): 
Proceeds with validating 
the survey at the district 
level. 
 No (red button): Skips the 
district-level validation and 
continues to the next step. 
 
 
This marks the completion of the Second level of the validation process for rural areas. 
 
After validation at the district level, the waste pickers survey cycle for rural areas is complete, and the 
waste pickers and waste workers' unique ID will be generated. Now they are eligible to receive benefits 
under the NAMASTE Scheme.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      71 
Annexure I: Validation Criteria 
Waste Picker Name 
Valid Invalid 
1. Name details matched with ULB/ 
Block/ GP level list (desk 
verification) 
2. Met the respondent and 
validated through a proof of 
identity (Aadhar card, Voter ID, 
Ration card, Waste Picker ID, Bank 
Passbook, Driving license, Any 
authorised letter) 
3. Met the respondent and 
validated through interview (no 
proof of identity was available) 
1. Did not meet the respondent and validated 
through interview of community member/ 
validated Waste Picker  
2. Did not meet the respondent and validated 
through interview of ULB/Block/GP field level 
staff (Swachhagrahi, Swachh Saathi/ SWM 
Supervisor/ Sanitary Inspector/ B 
3. lock Sanitation Officer etc.) 
4. Person not found 
 
Job role 
Valid Invalid 
1. Met the respondent and validated 
through a proof of occupation 
furnished by the respondent (Job ID 
card, contract, salary slip, Waste 
Picker ID, receipt of sale etc.) 
2.  Details matched with the list collated 
by the ULB Nodal Officer with the 
help of NGO, CBO, ROs and Trade 
Union. 
3. Physical Verification by the 
ULB/Block/GP Nodal Officer 
1. Did not meet the respondent and validated through 
interview of community member/ validated Waste 
Picker/representative from NGO 
2. Did not meet the respondent and validated through 
interview of ULB/ Block/ GP field level staff 
(Swachhagrahi, Swachh Saathi, / SWM Supervisor/ 
Sanitary Inspector/ Block Sanitation Officer, Panchayat 
Secretary, Block Resource Person etc.) 
3. Family member of Waste Picker, not performing 
sanitation work themselves 
4. Working as a street sweeper on the payroll of ULB and 
picking waste as part of that job (rejected as the 
person is on the payroll as a Sweeper. 
5. Did not meet the respondent and rejected through 
interview of community member/ validated Waste 
Picker 
6. Did not meet the respondent and was rejected through 
interview of ULB/ Block/ GP field level staff 
(Swachhagrahi, Swachh Saathi/ SWM Supervisor/ 
Sanitary Inspector/ Block Sanitation Officer etc.) 
7. Not performing waste picking/collecting/sorting - 
working as a supervisor, or in another job role 
8. Person not found 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Waste Pickers’ Survey - User Manual for Rural Areas                                                                                                      72

October 1st, 2024            Certificate No: A3S/ASAC/2425/0086  
 
WEB APPLICATION SECURITY AUDIT (SAFE TO HOST) CERTIFICATE 
 
 
Organization Name: NSKFDC 
Application Name Namaste Portal 
Site URL: https://namastescheme.dosje.gov.in/ 
Test Site URL: https://namaste.uat-projects.com/ 
Security Testing Start Date: 2nd August, 2024 
Security Testing End Date 
Hash Value 
1st October, 2024 
5635fa4ccc56012c225a22f27eebb5f57bf6485ef69a59d7b50e2883663e3557                           
 
This is to certify that the security audit of Namaste Portal Web Application as mentioned above has been 
completed and it is safe to host as on the date of this certificate. 
  
Observation The above site has undergone security audit as per OWASP web application security 
standard and for known web application vulnerabilities and has been certified to be safe 
to host. The content audited were based on the temporary site URL mentioned above. 
 
Recommendation: 
 
1. Site may be hosted with the permission of read only for general public 
2. Entire website should be hosted over valid SSL  
3. Web server and OS level hardening to be in place on the production server  
4. All the software components used in the application should be of latest and stable 
version.  
 
The validity of the certificate will lapse in case of changes to the application or 1 year from date of certificate, whichever is 
earlier. 
 
Authorized Signature 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Sagar Gupta

(So far, orders for 85,743 PPE kits and 690 ERSU safety kits have been placed.) Last Updated On: 05 May, 2026 Wastepicker Dashboard Analytics WastePicker Profiled: 226208 Gender Distribution : ( 133006 ) | ( 115000 ) | ( 24 ) Training Providers : 0 Resource Organization : 33 Batches : 0 Trainees Completed : 0 NHA Data : 0 State/UT Covered 34 Training Provider 0 Resource Organization 33 Batches 0 Total Trainee 0 Total NHA 0 Gender Distribution: (133006) | (115000) | (24) WastePicker Profiled 248030 our goals The goal of NAMASTE Scheme is to ensure safety and dignity of sanitation workers in India by creating an enabling ecosystem that recognizes sanitation workers as one of the key contributors in operations and maintenance of sanitation infrastructure there by providing sustainable livelihood and enhancing their occupational safety through capacity building and improved access to safety gear and machines. Self-Declaration For Self-Declaration form Identification of the person engaged in manual Scavenging Downlaod Swachhata Abhiyaan App Now Check Your Application Form Status × Check Status Status Remarks Close Submit National Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem (NAMASTE) Provision of PPE for SSWs Read More Provision of Safety Devices to ULBs Read More Workshops on Hazardous Cleaning of Sewers and Septic Tanks Read More Mechanised Cleaning Equipment/Machines Read More Profiling of Sewer Entry Professionals/Septic Tank Workers Read More Extending Health Insurance Scheme Benefits Read More Occupational Training Read More Provision of PPE for SSWs Read More Provision of Safety Devices to ULBs Read More Workshops on Hazardous Cleaning of Sewers and Septic Tanks Read More Mechanised Cleaning Equipment/Machines Read More Profiling of Sewer Entry Professionals/Septic Tank Workers Read More Extending Health Insurance Scheme Benefits Read More Occupational Training Read More Provision of PPE for SSWs Read More Our Social Media Our Gallery All SSW PPE Kit Miscellaneous SUY View all Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1) What does ‘NAMASTE’ stand for? NAMASTE is an abbreviation for “National Action for Mechanised Sanitation Ecosystem”. 3) What are the intended outcomes of the NAMASTE scheme? The intended outcomes of the NAMASTE scheme include zero fatalities in sanitation work, formalization and skilling of sanitation workers, elimination of direct contact with human faecal matter, establishment, strengthening and capacitating of Emergency Response Sanitation Units (ERSUs), and empowerment of sanitation workers through self-help groups and entrepreneurship, access to alternate livelihood options, and occupational safety training to all sewer and septic tank workers (SSWs). 5) Who is a PSSO? PSSO is Private Sanitation Service Organisation who are involved in cleaning and maintenance of sewer and septic tanks and may or may not be empanelled/registered/contracted/licensed with ULB. 2) What is the objective of the NAMASTE scheme? 1. Ensure safety and dignity of sanitation workers in urban India and enhancing their occupational safety through capacity building and improved access to PPT Kits, safety devices and machines. 2. In addition, NAMASTE would bring about a behavior change amongst citizens towards sanitation workers and enhance demand for safe sanitation services. 3. Now waste pickers have also been included in Namaste scheme from the current financial year and through the scheme, they will be included in the solid waste management process by ensuring their safety and dignity. 4) Who is a Sewer and Septic tank worker (SSW)? Sanitation workers who are engaged in cleaning and maintenance of sewers including those managing pumping stations, manholes/machine holes, and sewer lines; and those engaged in septic tank cleaning. Such workers may or may not be engaged by ULB/parastatal, empanelled/registered/licensed Private Sanitation Service Organisation (PSSO) and other private sanitation service providers. 6) What is the role of State Government in implementation of NAMASTE scheme? The role of State Government in implementation of NAMASTE is very important as State Government is to ensure the implementation of the Prohibition of Employment as Manual Scavangers and their Rehabilitation Act, 2013 which prohibits hazardous cleaning of sewer and septic tanks. The State should have to immediately facilitate and issue notification for setting up of Emergency Response Sanitation Unit (ERSU) and designate Responsible Sanitation Authority (RSA) in all Municipal Corporations and Districts with establishment of helpline number 14420. Further, necessary actions should be taken up by State Level Nodal Officer for NAMASTE to support convergence across the Swachh Bharat Mission (SBM), Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT), NAMASTE and Deendayal Antyoday Yojana – National Urban Livelihood Mission (DAY-NULM). The State has to nominate a State NAMASTE Nodal Officer, from the Urban Development Department, for implementation of NAMASTE. pages.text: NSKFDC Skip to Main Content Screen Reader Mode Home Screen Reader Screen Reader Use the accessibility options provided by namastescheme.com to enhance screen display and readability. These features allow users to adjust text spacing, change text size, and switch color schemes for improved visibility and usability. Changing the Text Size You can modify the size of the text on this website using the text size adjustment icons located at the top-right of each page. This functionality helps users with visual impairments read the content more comfortably. Decrease text size: Reduces the text size by one level. Normal text size: Resets to the default text size. Increase text size: Increases the text size by one level. Changing the Color Scheme To ensure content is clearly visible and readable, the site offers color scheme options that apply contrasting background and text colors. These options are also accessible at the top of each page. Change Content Language You can view the content of the website in your preferred language: हिंदी: Click on the "हिंदी" link at the top to view content in Hindi. English: Click on the "English" link to return to the English version. Screen Reader Compatibility namastescheme.com follows the Guidelines for Indian Government Websites (GIGW) and is fully compatible with screen readers. Visitors with visual impairments can access the website using assistive technologies. The website supports the following screen readers: JAWS (Job Access With Speech) NVDA (NonVisual Desktop Access) SAFA Supernova Window-Eyes If you experience any accessibility issues or have suggestions for improvement, please contact our support team at nskfdc-msje@nic.in . Original text Rate this translation Your feedback will be used to help improve Google Translate

Self-Declaration For Self-Declaration form Identification of the person engaged in manual Scavenging Downlaod Swachhata Abhiyaan App Now Check Your Application Form Status × Check Status Status Remarks Close Submit National Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem (NAMASTE) Provision of PPE for SSWs Read More Provision of Safety Devices to ULBs Read More Workshops on Hazardous Cleaning of Sewers and Septic Tanks Read More Mechanised Cleaning Equipment/Machines Read More Profiling of Sewer Entry Professionals/Septic Tank Workers Read More Extending Health Insurance Scheme Benefits Read More Occupational Training Read More Provision of PPE for SSWs Read More Provision of Safety Devices to ULBs Read More Workshops on Hazardous Cleaning of Sewers and Septic Tanks Read More Mechanised Cleaning Equipment/Machines Read More Profiling of Sewer Entry Professionals/Septic Tank Workers Read More Extending Health Insurance Scheme Benefits Read More Occupational Training Read More Provision of PPE for SSWs Read More Our Social Media Our Gallery All SSW PPE Kit Miscellaneous SUY View all Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1) What does ‘NAMASTE’ stand for? NAMASTE is an abbreviation for “National Action for Mechanised Sanitation Ecosystem”. 3) What are the intended outcomes of the NAMASTE scheme? The intended outcomes of the NAMASTE scheme include zero fatalities in sanitation work, formalization and skilling of sanitation workers, elimination of direct contact with human faecal matter, establishment, strengthening and capacitating of Emergency Response Sanitation Units (ERSUs), and empowerment of sanitation workers through self-help groups and entrepreneurship, access to alternate livelihood options, and occupational safety training to all sewer and septic tank workers (SSWs). 5) Who is a PSSO? PSSO is Private Sanitation Service Organisation who are involved in cleaning and maintenance of sewer and septic tanks and may or may not be empanelled/registered/contracted/licensed with ULB. 2) What is the objective of the NAMASTE scheme? 1. Ensure safety and dignity of sanitation workers in urban India and enhancing their occupational safety through capacity building and improved access to PPT Kits, safety devices and machines. 2. In addition, NAMASTE would bring about a behavior change amongst citizens towards sanitation workers and enhance demand for safe sanitation services. 3. Now waste pickers have also been included in Namaste scheme from the current financial year and through the scheme, they will be included in the solid waste management process by ensuring their safety and dignity. 4) Who is a Sewer and Septic tank worker (SSW)? Sanitation workers who are engaged in cleaning and maintenance of sewers including those managing pumping stations, manholes/machine holes, and sewer lines; and those engaged in septic tank cleaning. Such workers may or may not be engaged by ULB/parastatal, empanelled/registered/licensed Private Sanitation Service Organisation (PSSO) and other private sanitation service providers. 6) What is the role of State Government in implementation of NAMASTE scheme? The role of State Government in implementation of NAMASTE is very important as State Government is to ensure the implementation of the Prohibition of Employment as Manual Scavangers and their Rehabilitation Act, 2013 which prohibits hazardous cleaning of sewer and septic tanks. The State should have to immediately facilitate and issue notification for setting up of Emergency Response Sanitation Unit (ERSU) and designate Responsible Sanitation Authority (RSA) in all Municipal Corporations and Districts with establishment of helpline number 14420. Further, necessary actions should be taken up by State Level Nodal Officer for NAMASTE to support convergence across the Swachh Bharat Mission (SBM), Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT), NAMASTE and Deendayal Antyoday Yojana – National Urban Livelihood Mission (DAY-NULM). The State has to nominate a State NAMASTE Nodal Officer, from the Urban Development Department, for implementation of NAMASTE. pages.title: NSKFDC
pages.text: NSKFDC Skip to Main Content Screen Reader Mode Home About Scheme National Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem (NAMASTE) Components of NAMASTE Scheme Profiling of Sewer Entry Professionals/Septic Tank Workers (SSWs). Health Insurance coverage of SSWs under PM-JAY. Training of SSWs, Sewer Entry Professionals, and Duty Supervisors. Capital Subsidy on Loans to SSWs for procurement of Sanitation Related Vehicles/Equipment through Swachhta Udyami Yojana. Provision of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) for SSWs. The Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment (MSJE) has formulated a scheme “National Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem” (NAMASTE). The process of extending the Scheme to all the Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) of the country has been initiated. The main features of the Scheme to be implemented in all ULBs are as follows: Identification: NAMASTE envisions identifying Sewer/Septic Tank Workers (SSWs). Occupational Training and distribution of PPE Kits to SSWs. Assistance for Safety Devices to Sanitation Response Units (SRUs). Extending Health Insurance Scheme Benefits to identified SSWs and their families under the Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY). Livelihood Assistance: The Action Plan will promote mechanization and enterprise development by providing funding support and subsidy (capital + interest) to sanitation workers, to procure sanitation-related equipment. IEC Campaign: Massive campaigns would be undertaken jointly by the ULBs & NSKFDC to spread awareness about the interventions of NAMASTE. NAMASTE SCHEME Profiling of Sewer Entry Professionals/Septic Tank Workers Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) and parastatal agencies will compile a list of Sanitation Workers (SSWs) employed by them. These workers will be verified during profiling camps conducted by ULBs, collecting additional details such as information about their dependents and their access to entitlements. The complete profile will be made available on the NAMASTE portal. Extending Health Insurance Scheme Benefits To provide safety nets for workers and their families in case of illness or accidents, SSWs and their families, as well as identified manual scavengers and their families, will be covered under the Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PM-JAY) by the National Health Authority. Occupational Training Sanitation Workers will undergo capacity building programs to equip them with modern sanitation techniques, awareness on occupational hazards, and usage of mechanized tools and equipment. This will enable a shift from unsafe manual practices to safe and dignified work environments. Provision of PPE for SSWs Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) kits will be distributed to SSWs to ensure their safety while performing sanitation-related tasks. These kits will include gloves, masks, boots, and other safety gear necessary for safe handling of waste and hazardous materials. Provision of Safety Devices to ULBs ULBs will be provided with modern safety devices and equipment to ensure mechanized cleaning of sewers and septic tanks. This initiative aims to eliminate hazardous manual cleaning and promote safe and sustainable practices. Workshops on Hazardous Cleaning of Sewers and Septic Tanks Workshops and training sessions will be organized to spread awareness about the risks of manual scavenging and the need for mechanization. Stakeholders will be educated about best practices in sanitation management and safe alternatives to hazardous cleaning. Mechanised Cleaning Equipment/Machines The scheme promotes adoption of mechanized cleaning equipment such as suction machines, jetting machines, and robotic tools to replace hazardous manual scavenging and ensure the safety and dignity of sanitation workers. Goals and Objectives The NAMASTE web portal and mobile apps serve as a centralized system for the NAMASTE scheme, offering comprehensive support through multiple user roles. Designed to enhance efficiency, the platform facilitates seamless management, streamlined data exchange, and robust reporting capabilities. The user-friendly interface of both the web portal and mobile apps aims to optimize user experience, fostering accessibility and engagement among participants in the NAMASTE scheme.
